content.text:
{
  "module": "auth-rbac",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 38,
    "domain_depth": 42,
    "data_richness": 30,
    "interaction_quality": 35,
    "ai_integration": 5,
    "mobile_first": 28,
    "performance": 40,
    "typescript_quality": 52,
    "error_handling": 32,
    "realtime": 20,
    "security": 45,
    "differentiation": 25,
    "enterprise_readiness": 35,
    "accessibility": 22
  },
  "overall_score": 32,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Login page missing error message mapping for Supabase auth errors",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page either displays raw Supabase error strings like 'Invalid login credentials' or silently swallows auth errors. Per AUTH-002 and AUTH-007 verification tests, errors must be mapped to user friendly messages. Users see technical error codes instead of actionable guidance.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the login form submission handler (the function that calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword or equivalent): BEFORE: The error from Supabase auth is displayed directly or not displayed at all. The raw error message like 'Invalid login credentials' or 'AuthApiError' may leak to the UI. AFTER: Add an error mapping function at the top of the file: function mapAuthError(message: string): string { const msg = message.toLowerCase(); if (msg.includes('invalid login') || msg.includes('invalid_credentials')) return 'Email or password is incorrect'; if (msg.includes('email not confirmed')) return 'Please check your email to confirm your account'; if (msg.includes('rate limit') || msg.includes('too many')) return 'Too many attempts. Please try again in a few minutes'; if (msg.includes('fetch') || msg.includes('network') || msg.includes('failed to fetch')) return 'Unable to connect. Check your internet connection'; if (msg.includes('already registered') || msg.includes('already been registered')) return 'An account with this email already exists'; if (msg.includes('password') && msg.includes('short')) return 'Password must be at least 6 characters'; return 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'; } In the catch/error handler, call mapAuthError(error.message) before setting the error state. Ensure the error is rendered in a visible div with role='alert' aria-live='assertive' and styled with color red (theme.colors.statusDanger or '#E74C3C'). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute renders children briefly before auth check completes",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The ProtectedRoute component has a loading state skeleton, but the isDevBypassActive() check and the conditional logic may allow a flash of protected content before the auth state resolves. The loading check must be the absolute first gate before any children render, and the Navigate to /login must preserve the returnTo path for post login redirect.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the default exported component (the main ProtectedRoute function component): BEFORE: The component may render children before the auth loading state fully resolves, or the redirect to /login may not include the current path as a returnTo query parameter. The loading skeleton may not show during the initial auth check. AFTER: Ensure the component follows this exact order: (1) If isDevBypassActive() is true, render DevBanner + children and return early. (2) If auth is loading (user is null AND session has not been checked yet), render the SkeletonLoader with aria-label 'Verifying authentication' and return. (3) If user is null and not loading, return <Navigate to={`/login?returnTo=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname + location.search)}`} replace />. (4) If moduleId is provided, check permissions via usePermissions and if denied render RequestAccessPage with the moduleName prop. (5) Only after all gates pass, render children. Ensure useLocation() is imported from react-router-dom and called at the top of the component. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Login form missing ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation support",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page lacks form labels associated with inputs via htmlFor/id pairing, has no aria-required on required fields, no aria-describedby for error messages, and no visible focus rings. Per Global Acceptance Standards, all forms must be keyboard navigable with visible focus indicators and screen reader support.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the login form JSX: BEFORE: Input fields may use placeholder text instead of visible labels, lack id attributes paired with htmlFor on labels, have no aria-required='true' on required fields, and error messages are not linked to inputs via aria-describedby. The submit button may lack a disabled state during loading. AFTER: (1) Add a visible <label htmlFor='login-email' style={{ fontSize: '12px', color: '#6B7280', fontWeight: 500, marginBottom: '4px', display: 'block' }}>Email address</label> above the email input. Set id='login-email' and aria-required='true' on the input. (2) Add a visible <label htmlFor='login-password'>Password</label> above the password input with id='login-password' and aria-required='true'. (3) If there is an error state, render it in a <div id='login-error' role='alert' aria-live='assertive'> and add aria-describedby='login-error' to the relevant input. (4) Add aria-busy='true' and disabled to the submit button when the form is submitting. (5) Ensure all inputs have style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }} with an onFocus handler that adds a '0 0 0 2px #F47820' boxShadow for visible focus rings. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Login page not responsive at 375px width, touch targets too small",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page layout does not adapt to mobile viewport (375px iPhone SE). Input fields and buttons may overflow or be too small for touch interaction. Per design standards, touch targets must be minimum 44x44px and the page must be usable at 375px with no horizontal scroll.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the outermost container div and form elements: BEFORE: The login form container may have a fixed width that causes horizontal scroll on mobile, input heights may be less than 44px, and the submit button may be smaller than 44px tall. The form may not be centered vertically on mobile. AFTER: (1) Set the outermost container to style={{ minHeight: '100vh', display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center', padding: '16px', boxSizing: 'border-box' }}. (2) Set the form wrapper to style={{ width: '100%', maxWidth: '400px' }}. (3) Ensure all input elements have style that includes minHeight: '44px', fontSize: '16px' (prevents iOS zoom on focus), padding: '12px 16px', width: '100%', boxSizing: 'border-box'. (4) Ensure the submit button has minHeight: '44px', width: '100%', fontSize: '16px', fontWeight: 600. (5) Add a meta viewport check or ensure no element exceeds 100% width. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "PermissionGate loading state causes layout shift and has no skeleton animation",
      "file": "src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx",
      "description": "When showDenied is true and permissions are loading, the PermissionGate renders a static gray div with a CSS animation reference to 'pulse' that is not defined in inline styles. This causes either no animation or a console error. The skeleton should use a proper inline keyframe or opacity transition.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the loading branch where showDenied is true (the div with animation: 'pulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite'): BEFORE: The skeleton div references a CSS animation called 'pulse' via the animation property, but since we use inline styles only (no CSS files), this animation is never defined and the element appears as a static gray rectangle. AFTER: Remove the animation property from the inline style. Instead, add a simple opacity-based loading indicator by creating a small React state inside the PermissionGate that toggles opacity. Alternatively, simply set opacity: 0.6 as a static value to indicate loading state without animation, since this is a brief transient state. Change the style to: { width: '100%', height: '32px', backgroundColor: colors.surfaceInset, borderRadius: borderRadius.md, opacity: 0.6 }. This avoids referencing undefined CSS keyframes while still visually indicating a loading state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore requires multi day training for role setup. Fieldwire has no role based access at all. SiteSync has the permission infrastructure (PermissionGate, ProtectedRoute, usePermissions) but the login UX and error handling gaps would cause immediate user frustration during onboarding. The dev bypass mechanism is good for development velocity but must be tightened before any pilot. The construction specific role hierarchy (owner, gc_admin, superintendent, foreman, subcontractor) is correctly modeled in usePermissions, which is ahead of Buildertrend's simple admin/user split.",
  "summary": "The Authentication and Authorization module has solid architectural foundations: PermissionGate, ProtectedRoute, usePermissions hook with construction specific roles, and dev bypass for rapid development. However, five critical gaps block production readiness. The Login page leaks raw Supabase error strings to users, lacks ARIA attributes for accessibility compliance, and is not responsive at mobile widths (violating the field first principle). The ProtectedRoute may flash protected content before auth resolves and does not preserve returnTo paths for post login redirect. The PermissionGate references undefined CSS keyframes. These are all surgical fixes targeting existing code, not new features. Fixing these five issues would raise the module from 32 to approximately 50, establishing a stable auth baseline for the Architect mode features (email notifications, MFA, invite flow) defined in the Production Roadmap."
}
